nodes.label: AI as coding suggestion tool
nodes.grounding_span: I mostly use it for coding tasks, more specifically in explaining/researching code in a larger context, rather than creating code

nodes.label: AI as explanation aid for known concepts
nodes.grounding_span: I've seen it hallucinate when trying to write the actual code, more than when I use it for explanation

nodes.label: AI as efficiency tool
nodes.grounding_span: I do not think that AI is something that 'would take over', but rather something that I can use to be more efficient

nodes.label: AI as confidence-building assistant
nodes.label_negative: Low confidence tasks delegated to AI
nodes.grounding_span: If the task is a one-liner or something I am already 100% confident I can do myself, I usually skip AI

nodes.label: AI requiring context explanation
nodes.grounding_span: I feel like it has not captured the entire picture

nodes.label: Efficiency and time savings
nodes.grounding_span: it saves time for some time-consuming tasks

nodes.label: Cognitive clarity and understanding
nodes.grounding_span: it made it clear for me in a few minutes

nodes.label: Control and reliability in work outcomes
nodes.grounding_span: the implications are rather severe if something messes up

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI as a helpful tool
nodes.grounding_span: I felt really satisfied by the use of AI in this case

nodes.label: Distrust due to hallucination experience
nodes.grounding_span: I've seen it hallucinate when trying to write the actual code

nodes.label: Optimism about AI as future tool
nodes.grounding_span: I would probably use it more if it gets better at writing code

nodes.label: Skepticism and caution toward AI
nodes.grounding_span: I feel like it has not captured the entire picture

nodes.label: Iterative back-and-forth questioning
nodes.grounding_span: I usually ask about 2-3 turns of follow-up questions

nodes.label: Strategic delegation with oversight
nodes.grounding_span: I never delegate entire tasks to AI. I probably use AI for like 30% of the task
